betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Visual Displays: Screens & Keyboards§f

§aEstablish a visual interface to display status readouts and interact with your programs!§f
Computers are headless by default. To actually view code and output, you must link your computer to a screen and keyboard.

§6Screen Tiers & Resolution§f:
§b• Tier 1 Screen§f: Supports monochrome (black & white) displays at a resolution of 50×16.
§b• Tier 2 Screen§f: Supports a 16-color palette at a resolution of 80×25.
§b• Tier 3 Screen§f: Supports full 256-color depth at a resolution of 160×50.

§6Multi-block Screens§f:
You can build large visual arrays by placing screens adjacent to each other. Screens will automatically merge into a single large §bMulti-block Display§f. 
§cNote§f: Each individual screen block in a multi-block setup consumes §e0.05 energy per tick§f while active. Larger arrays drain power significantly faster!

§6Keyboards & Input§f:
Screens are purely output devices. To type commands and write programs, you must craft a §bKeyboard§f and place it on one of the faces of your screen. 
§9Interaction Controls§f:
§9• Open Terminal§f: Right-click the screen (with keyboard attached) to open the interactive command console.
§9• Direct Touch§f: Tier 2 and Tier 3 screens support direct mouse interaction. You can click on GUI elements inside programs without needing a keyboard.
